calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2024 Continued To Sep-25-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, and RASIER-CA, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Action.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC and Rasier-CA, LLC's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Plaintiff Marygrace Anaya agreed to arbitrate disputes with Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc. (See Ex. G, Section (2), subparts (a)(1) & (a)(4).) And the arbitration agreement appears to encompass the instant dispute. The parties delegated issues of arbitrability to the arbitrator. (See ADR Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedure, Rule 8.) Thus, an order compelling arbitration appears proper. Plaintiff, in opposition, has not demonstrated that arbitration is not in order.

Plaintiff cites no authority holding that an arbitration agreement (including a delegation clause) is unenforceable merely because the arbitrator is not expressly and individually listed by name. Such a rule is not feasible given people's schedules, and one cannot foresee when a claim will arise. The parties' agreement that ADR Services, Inc. will conduct the arbitration is sufficient. (Gaddis Decl., Ex. G, sec. 2(c).)

Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2, subdivision (c) ("section 1281.2(c)") is not a basis to deny arbitration. First, there is no third-party litigation: Kenneth Allen Johnson and Ben Pan Kau Ng are not parties to any litigation related to this dispute. In addition, the Federal Arbitration Act ("FAA") governs this dispute. (Gaddis Decl., Ex. G, sec. 2(c).) Accordingly, section 1281.2(c) does not apply to this case. (Victrola 89, LLC v. Jaman Properties 8 LLC (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 337, 355 ["[A] state procedural statute or rule that frustrated the effectuation of section 2 [of the FAA]'s central policy would, where the federal law applied, be preempted by the [FAA]."]; Rodriguez v. Am. Techs., Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1116, 1121-1122.) In any event, on this record, the court declines to exercise its discretion to refuse Uber's arbitration demand.

Having granted the motion to compel arbitration, the court stays this proceeding pending the arbitration. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 1281.4.)

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
